content:
Apple Watch is gaining a new watch face with the watchOS 26.5 software update. The new option is an analog watch face with a wide range of personalization options. It can be as colorful or as monochromatic as you’d like.

Pride Luminance watch face lets you create your own design with custom color combinations

The new Pride Luminance watch face can take on several different styles and shapes with countless color combinations. It’s an analog watch face that always displays the ticking seconds hand on Apple Watch Series 10, Series 11, and Ultra 3.

Every Apple Watch running watchOS 26.5 can customize several aspects of the new Pride Luminance face.

You can add one of the pre-configured designs from the Face Gallery or get creative and configure your own. Here’s the watch face description:

The Pride Luminance watch face celebrates the vibrancy, spirit, and individuality of the LGBTQ+ community. For the first time, you can choose background colors from various Pride flags to create a unique expression. A dynamic glass layer sits on top of the background and rotates in unison with the minute hand. The effect is one of joyful motion and multifaceted self-expression.

Customization options include color, dial shape, style, and corner complications for the circular dial shape.

Apple provides 11 pre-configured color combinations. You can also pick from over 70 colors, choosing any combination of one to 12 colors as a time.

Like a lot of other analog watch faces, dial options include rectangle for a full screen background or circle to allow corner complications.

The two style options include one that radiates out from the center and another that features six vertical lines.

When paired with the circle dial option, the new watch face supports up to four corner complications.

Each watch face uses white hour and minute hands while the seconds hand changes based on color choices.

The new Pride Luminance face arrives as part of watchOS 26.5.

Apple similarly offers a highly customizable wallpaper version on iPhone and iPad in iOS 26.5. The new software updates are currently in beta and should arrive during the second week of May.

You can find the new Apple Watch Series 11 for as low as $299 on Amazon today.
